title: Gomphocarpus corniculatus
taxonomy: Gomphocarpus corniculatus is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Furthermore, it is also known to be within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a part of the Apocynaceae family. As a species within the genus Gomphocarpus, it is closely related to other species within the same genus. Its classification highlights its evolutionary relationship with other plants within the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Gomphocarpus corniculatus, commonly known as the hairy bindweed or hairy giant milkweed, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, wounds, and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use. The plant's extracts may also have potential in the development of new pharmaceuticals, but further studies are required.

constituents: Gomphocarpus corniculatus, also known as devil's cotton, contains various active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides. These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's seeds are particularly rich in cardenolides, which are responsible for its emetic properties. intro: Gomphocarpus cornicarpus is a plant species commonly known as Balloon Cotton Bush or Balloon Milkweed, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is rich in health benefits, particularly in its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Gomphocarpus corniculatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the form of tinctures and capsules to harness its health benefits.
